scenario: A fintech team at Lattice Bank is preparing 12 DMN 1.3 decision models in Camunda 8.8 Web Modeler before a release. The lead onboards 5 new modelers who need a clear understanding of where a DMN modeling session begins in the tool. The platform serves 12 tenants and processes 8400 transactions per day.
question: Which view does a DMN modeling session start in for Camunda 8.8 Desktop or Web Modeler?

options.text: Decision Requirements Diagram view where DMN elements are added from the palette on the left side of the canvas
Decision table view where rows and columns are edited directly without first creating a containing DRD diagram
Properties panel view where every DMN element must be named before any modeling canvas opens for editing
Overview panel view where existing decision tables are listed before any DRD canvas becomes available to edit

a.text: Correct. The DMN docs state that modeling for DMN 1.3 models starts in the Decision Requirements Diagram view, from which DMN elements are added by dragging them out of the palette on the left side.

b.text: Incorrect. A decision table is edited only after a decision has been morphed into a table, by clicking the overlay on the upper-left corner of the decision inside the DRD view.

c.text: Incorrect. The properties panel on the right side is used to change the name or ID of the DMN diagram, not as the entry point for a modeling session.

d.text: Incorrect. The Overview lets the modeler jump between decision tables and literal expressions inside an existing DRD, but it is not where a fresh modeling session starts.

explanation: DMN modeling in Camunda 8.8 Desktop or Web Modeler begins in the Decision Requirements Diagram view, where elements are added by dragging them from the palette on the left side of the canvas onto the diagram.
